meta.name: Denmark
meta.aboutStudying: Denmark offers free tuition for EU/EEA students and a generous state grant (SU) for those who work part-time. Danish universities are world-renowned for design, engineering and life sciences, and almost every Master's programme is taught in English. The famous Danish 'hygge' culture and excellent work-life balance make it one of Europe's happiest study destinations.

tips.title: Free for EU Students
tips.text: EU/EEA students pay no tuition fees in Denmark. If you take a part-time job (which most students do), you can also claim the Danish state education grant (SU) of around DKK 6,800/month. Non-EU students pay DKK 45,000–120,000/year.

tips.title: Cycling Capital
tips.text: Copenhagen is the world's most bike-friendly city — over 60% of residents cycle to work or study daily. A bike is essential and far cheaper than public transport. The flat terrain and superb cycle lanes make getting around effortless year-round.

tips.title: Hygge & Balance
tips.text: Danish study culture values group work, flat hierarchies and balance over competition. The concept of 'hygge' (cosy contentment) permeates everything. Expect to call professors by their first name and to enjoy genuinely sociable, low-stress academics.

universities.name: University of Copenhagen
universities.desc: Denmark's oldest and highest-ranked university — a global top-100 institution and home to numerous Nobel laureates.

universities.name: Technical University of Denmark
universities.desc: One of Europe's leading engineering universities — consistently ranked among the top technical schools on the continent.

universities.name: Aarhus University
universities.desc: A QS top-100 university set on a beautiful park campus, with the highly ranked Aarhus BSS business school.

universities.name: Aalborg University
universities.desc: World-leader in problem-based learning — every programme is built around real-world project work with industry.

universities.name: University of Southern Denmark
universities.desc: A modern multi-campus university with a strong reputation for robotics, engineering and health sciences.

universities.name: Copenhagen Business School
universities.desc: One of the largest and most respected business schools in Europe, with triple-crown accreditation and global rankings.

universities.name: Roskilde University
universities.desc: A progressive, interdisciplinary university built entirely on project-based and group learning.

universities.name: IT University of Copenhagen
universities.desc: A specialist IT university producing many of Scandinavia's top software engineers and game developers.

Copenhagen.desc: Consistently ranked among the world's most liveable cities — Copenhagen pairs Scandinavian design, cycling culture and waterfront living with two of Denmark's best universities.
Copenhagen.studentLife: Outstanding — large international student body, bike everywhere, harbour swimming in summer.
Copenhagen.transport: World-class metro, cycling network and S-trains; airport 15 min from centre.
Copenhagen.nightlife: Sophisticated bar and club scene around Vesterbro and Nørrebro.

Aarhus.desc: Denmark's second city and a true student capital — younger, cheaper and more relaxed than Copenhagen, with a famously vibrant cultural scene.
Aarhus.studentLife: Exceptional — one in seven residents is a student; huge social and music scene.
Aarhus.transport: New light rail, extensive buses and a compact, walkable centre.
Aarhus.nightlife: Lively and affordable — the Latin Quarter is packed with student bars.

Odense.desc: The birthplace of Hans Christian Andersen — a charming, green and very affordable city with a fast-growing robotics and engineering sector.
highlights: University of Southern Denmark robotics labs
Odense.studentLife: Relaxed and friendly — a manageable city ideal for focused study.
Odense.transport: New light rail and excellent cycling; 75 min to Copenhagen by train.
Odense.nightlife: Modest but pleasant — cosy bars in the historic centre.

Aalborg.desc: A reinvented industrial city in northern Denmark — now a buzzing, affordable student town built around Aalborg University's project-based learning model.
highlights: Utzon Center (by the Sydney Opera architect)
Aalborg.studentLife: Strong — AAU's group-work culture creates tight student communities.
Aalborg.transport: Compact and bike-friendly; airport and trains south to the rest of Denmark.
Aalborg.nightlife: Famous for Jomfru Ane Gade, one of Scandinavia's longest bar streets.

cityCards.desc: One of the world's most liveable cities — design, cycling and two of Denmark's best universities.
Denmark's vibrant second city — younger, cheaper and a true student capital.
Hans Christian Andersen's hometown — affordable, green and a growing robotics hub.
A reinvented industrial city, now a buzzing and affordable student town.
